stop.name: 14, King Street Wc2
stop.reason: 14, King Street Wc2 is a building that might contribute to the area's hidden alley character.

story:
This quiet stretch is bordered by hidden gems: 30m away is [St Paul's, Covent Garden](https://www.google.com/maps/search/St+Paul%27s%2C+Covent+Garden/@51.51138888888889,-0.12416666666666668,17z/), affectionately known as 'The Actor’s Church' – London’s first purpose-built Protestant church, and a sanctuary for thespians since the 17th century.

🎭 St Paul’s is more than architecture – it's a stage. Its adjacent [St Paul Covent Garden](https://www.google.com/maps/search/St+Paul+Covent+Garden/@51.51166666666666,-0.12361111111111112,17z/) gardens transform into an open-air theatre in summer, proving theatrics thrive even in flower-stalls. Beneath the grandeur? Forgotten corners of King Street still hum with the ghosts of actors, hipsters, and Inigo Jones’ original design. 

🤫 Skip the tourist trail – here, the real drama is in the quiet. [Peek inside the church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Paul%27s,_Covent+Garden) or let the alleyways whisper their tales. You might even imagine a secret passageway between the Lamb and St Paul – a hidden link for late-night soliloquies.
